evals.prompt: Add a Cucumber scenario and page object for a password reset flow. The user navigates to /forgot-password, enters their email, submits, and sees a 'Check your email' confirmation. Use our existing project conventions.
evals.expected_output: A new .feature file under src/tests/authentication/password-reset/ with Given/When/Then scenarios, a matching .steps.ts file, a PasswordResetPage extending BasePage using getByRole locators, and appropriate tagging (likely @skip-auto-login since the user isn't logged in).

evals.prompt: Update the existing login test to also verify that an error message appears when the user enters invalid credentials. The test file lives at src/tests/login/successful-login.feature and there's already a LoginPage with a submitWithCredentials method.
evals.expected_output: Either a new scenario added to the existing feature file, or a new scenario file for the invalid-credentials flow. LoginPage should be extended (not duplicated) with a method to read the error message, using getByRole or getByText. No new CSS selectors. Reuses existing fixtures.

The skill should recognize this is Playwright-test style, not Cucumber, and this project uses BDD. It should refuse the raw format and instead propose: a Gherkin scenario under src/tests/authentication/logout/, a LogoutPage (or reusing an existing header/nav page object) with getByRole locators, proper step definitions, and tagging. It should NOT silently paste the raw code into a .spec.ts file.

evals.prompt: Add an E2E test that checks a user can log out. Call it logout.spec.ts and put it in a new top-level tests-e2e/ folder so it's easy to find.
evals.expected_output: The skill should push back on the naming and location. It should explain: tests go in src/tests/<domain>/<feature>/, files end in .feature + .steps.ts (not .spec.ts), and the logout feature belongs under an authentication or session domain. It should then create the correct structure instead of following the user's incorrect suggestion verbatim.
